pushing_hardest_on: Success is directional — 'fall' and 'stops climbing' with no baseline, target, or threshold
The churn-to-flakiness causal claim is the load-bearing bet and it is asserted, not evidenced
The merchant-facing changes ship with no rollout or migration plan

axes.reason: Merchant trust is named as the organizing driver, but there is no segmentation and the churn it points to is never quantified.
axes.quote: stop losing merchants to flakiness before we chase new payment methods
axes.what_id_want_to_see: Which merchants churn (size, volume, vertical) and the measured churn rate the roadmap is trying to bend.

axes.reason: The Now bucket is concrete, correctly-scoped table-stakes engineering, tied directly to the failure it prevents.
axes.quote: Ship idempotency keys on the charge API and a real retry-with-backoff path.
axes.what_id_want_to_see: Already strong — an explicit acceptance bar (zero double-charges on retry) would close it out.

axes.reason: Now/Next/Later sequencing with a real reason for deferring new rails, but no explicit out-of-scope or CUT line for H2 itself.
axes.quote: Sequenced last on purpose — new rails on a flaky core just multiply the failure surface.
axes.what_id_want_to_see: An explicit 'not in H2' list so the Later bucket can't quietly pull forward.

axes.reason: Both success signals are directional only — 'fall' and 'stops climbing' carry no baseline, target, timeframe, or threshold, so the roadmap can't be falsified.
axes.quote: We will know H2 worked when outage minutes fall and merchant churn stops climbing.
axes.what_id_want_to_see: A baseline (outage minutes and churn today), a target with a date, and a threshold that would say H2 failed.

axes.reason: An internal reliability roadmap; no external pricing decision is at stake (the economics, if any, belong under Strategy).
A genuine sequencing thesis — earn trust before chasing expansion — and it is consistently applied across the Now/Next/Later split.
axes.quote: After two outages last quarter, the platform's job is to stop losing merchants to flakiness before we chase new payment methods.
axes.what_id_want_to_see: Already strong — name the cost of being wrong (what a competitor ships while we harden) to pressure-test the sequencing.

axes.reason: The Next bucket is merchant-facing (status page, webhook guarantees) but there is no rollout, comms, or migration plan for getting merchants onto it.
axes.quote: Add a merchant-facing status page and webhook delivery guarantees.
axes.what_id_want_to_see: A rollout plan for the merchant-facing changes — who gets the status page first, how webhook-contract changes are communicated and migrated.

axes.reason: Stage is explicit and honest — a mature platform with live merchants recovering from real outages, sequencing hardening ahead of new rails.
axes.quote: Explore alternative payment methods (bank debit, wallets) once the core is trustworthy.
axes.what_id_want_to_see: Already strong — a trust threshold that gates the Later bucket would make the stage-gating mechanical.

axes.reason: No AI/LLM feature is proposed in this roadmap.
axes.what_id_want_to_see: n/a — no model-backed feature in scope.

axes.reason: Names the sharpest technical risk (stale-webhook replay) and gestures at the mitigation, but offers no broader pre-mortem covering the outages themselves or merchant comms.
axes.quote: The redrive queue could replay stale webhooks and confuse downstream merchant systems if we don't dedupe on delivery.
axes.what_id_want_to_see: A pre-mortem beyond the redrive queue — repeat-outage blast radius, status-page accuracy during an incident, and the dedupe key's failure modes.

weakest_claims.claim: H2 is a success when outage minutes fall and churn stops climbing.
weakest_claims.quote: We will know H2 worked when outage minutes fall and merchant churn stops climbing.
followups: What are outage minutes and churn today, and what number by when counts as success?
If churn keeps climbing while outage minutes fall, did H2 work — or is flakiness not the real driver?

weakest_claims.claim: Merchants are leaving because of flakiness, so reliability is the right H2 bet.
weakest_claims.quote: stop losing merchants to flakiness before we chase new payment methods
followups: What evidence ties churn to reliability specifically, versus price, coverage, or missing payment methods?

strengths: Clear Now/Next/Later sequencing with a real reason for deferring new rails
An explicit trust-before-expansion thesis, consistently applied
Honest, well-chosen Now-bucket table-stakes tied to a concrete failure
must_dos: Put a baseline, dated target, and failure threshold on outage minutes and churn
Evidence that flakiness — not price or coverage — is why merchants actually churn
A rollout and migration plan for the merchant-facing status page and webhook changes

limits: No engineering design is linked — the redrive 'delivered' semantics and the dedupe key may be specified elsewhere, not visible in this roadmap
